AMZN: revenue + cost of revenue

2 topics recognized and researched independently
revenuecost of revenue
Topic 1 of 2

AMZN: revenue

4 quarter(s) searched · 66 filing section(s) searched · 4 period(s) with evidence
Credibility timeline
Q1 2022Q2 2022Q3 2022Q4 2022
guidance met/exceededguidance missedguidance given, not yet resolvedno forecast data this quarter

Longitudinal Summary

Over the period, revenue consistently met or exceeded guidance provided by management. Revenue growth was reported in each quarter, with increases ranging from 7% to 20% year-over-year. Unearned revenue was recognized progressively throughout the year, with significant amounts recognized in each quarter. Management highlighted specific factors impacting revenue growth, such as changes in foreign exchange rates and the contribution of events like Prime Day.

Forecasting Credibility: High

Management consistently provided accurate revenue guidance that was either met or exceeded in each reporting period, demonstrating a high level of forecasting credibility.

Trends

  • Revenue growth reported in each quarter
  • Consistent recognition of unearned revenue throughout the year

Recurring Themes / Risks

  • Impact of foreign exchange rates on revenue
  • Contribution of events like Prime Day to revenue growth

Key Concerns

  • No major inconsistencies or red flags identified in the revenue reporting

Conclusion

Throughout the year, revenue performance aligned with or surpassed management's guidance, indicating a strong forecasting track record. The consistent growth in revenue, coupled with the progressive recognition of unearned revenue, reflects a stable and reliable revenue stream for the company. Factors such as the impact of foreign exchange rates and the success of events like Prime Day continue to influence revenue trends.

By Reporting Period

Q1 2022

What management said

  • "Like, in Q1 literally, where revenue 61% higher over two years from 2020."
  • "On revenue, yes, the revenue guidance we've given is kind of our best view of what we're seeing right now."
  • "Brian Olsavsky And on revenue -- excuse me, advertising revenue was up 25% year-over-year, and that's a strong run rate compared to the revenue growth rate."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • from 10-K/10-Q"Our total unearned revenue as of December 31, 2021 was $ 14.0 billion, of which $ 5.1 billion was recognized as revenue during the three months ended March 31, 2022."
  • from 10-K/10-Q"Total video and music expense was $ 3.0 billion and $ 3.5 billion in Q1 2021 and Q1 2022."
  • from 10-K/10-Q"Sales increased 7% in Q1 2022 compared to the comparable prior year period."
  • "Brian Olsavsky And on revenue -- excuse me, advertising revenue was up 25% year-over-year, and that's a strong run rate compared to the revenue growth rate."
  • "Prime Day contributed about 400 basis points to our Q2 2021 year-over-year revenue growth rate."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

  • jump to section"Our total unearned revenue as of December 31, 2021 was $ 14.0 billion, of which $ 5.1 billion was recognized as revenue during the three months ended March 31, 2022."
  • jump to section"Unearned Revenue Unearned revenue is recorded when payments are received or due in advance of performing our service obligations and is recognized over the service period."
  • jump to section"Unearned revenue primarily relates to prepayments of AWS services and Amazon Prime memberships."
  • jump to section"However, the amount and timing of revenue recognition is largely driven by customer usage, which can extend beyond the original contractual term."
Risk Factors
Risk FactorsItem 1A.Risk Factors jump to section
Item 1A.Risk Factors
  • jump to section"Our revenue and operating profit growth depends on the continued growth of demand for the products and services offered by us or our sellers, and our business is affected by general economic and business conditions worldwide."

Item 1.Legal Proceedings
Q2 2022

What management said

  • "It sounds like you're going to have revenue up nicely."
  • "For the quarter, worldwide net sales of $121.2 billion exceeded the top end of our revenue guidance range and represented an increase of 10% year-over-year, excluding approximately 320 basis points of unfavorable impact from changes in foreign exchange rates."

Guidance given

  • "For revenue, note that our guidance includes an estimated approximately 390 basis points of unfavorable impact from year-over-year change in foreign exchange rates."

Actual reported

  • from 10-K/10-Q"Our total unearned revenue as of December 31, 2021 was $ 14.0 billion, of which $ 8.1 billion was recognized as revenue during the six months ended June 30, 2022."
  • "For the quarter, worldwide net sales of $121.2 billion exceeded the top end of our revenue guidance range and represented an increase of 10% year-over-year, excluding approximately 320 basis points of unfavorable impact from changes in foreign exchange rates."
  • "Prime Day also occurred in Q2 last year and contributed about 400 basis points to our Q2 2021 year-over-year revenue growth rate."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

+11 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"Our total unearned revenue as of December 31, 2021 was $ 14.0 billion, of which $ 8.1 billion was recognized as revenue during the six months ended June 30, 2022."
  • jump to section"Unearned Revenue Unearned revenue is recorded when payments are received or due in advance of performing our service obligations and is recognized over the service period."
  • jump to section"Unearned revenue primarily relates to prepayments of AWS services and Amazon Prime memberships."
  • jump to section"However, the amount and timing of revenue recognition is largely driven by customer usage, which can extend beyond the original contractual term."
Risk Factors
Risk FactorsItem 1A.Risk Factors jump to section
Item 1A.Risk Factors
  • jump to section"Our revenue and operating profit growth depends on the continued growth of demand for the products and services offered by us or our sellers, and our business is affected by general economic and business conditions worldwide."

What management said

No transcript mentions this period.

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • Metfrom 10-K/10-Qresolves AMZN_Q2_2022 guidance "Our total unearned revenue as of December 31, 2021 was $ 14.0 billion, of which $ 10.1 billion was recognized as revenue during the nine months ended September 30, 2022."
  • "Backlog has been running 60-plus percent, so the divergence between revenue and backlog is pretty large."
  • "We reported overall net income of $2.9 billion in the third quarter."

  • jump to section"Our total unearned revenue as of December 31, 2021 was $ 14.0 billion, of which $ 10.1 billion was recognized as revenue during the nine months ended September 30, 2022."
  • jump to section"Unearned Revenue Unearned revenue is recorded when payments are received or due in advance of performing our service obligations and is recognized over the service period."
  • jump to section"Unearned revenue primarily relates to prepayments of AWS services and Amazon Prime memberships."
  • jump to section"However, the amount and timing of revenue recognition is largely driven by customer usage, which can extend beyond the original contractual term."
Risk Factors
Risk FactorsItem 1A.Risk Factors jump to section
Item 1A.Risk Factors
  • jump to section"Our revenue and operating profit growth depends on the continued growth of demand for the products and services offered by us or our sellers, and our business is affected by general economic and business conditions worldwide."

Q4 2022

What management said

  • "Let's start with revenue."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • "We saw good growth in advertising revenues in Q4, up 23% year-over-year, excluding the impact of foreign exchange."
  • "Net sales increased $21.4 billion in Q4, up 20% year-over-year and now representing an annualized sales run rate of more than $85 billion."

Financial StatementsItem 8.Financial Statements and Supplementary Data jump to section
Uncertain Tax Positions
Description of
the Matter
As discussed in Notes 1 and 9 of the consolidated financial statements, the Company is subject to income taxes in the U.S. and numerous foreign jurisdictions and during the ordinary course of business, there are many tax positions for which the ultimate tax determination is uncertain. As a result, significant judgment is required in evaluating the Company’s tax positions and determining its provision for income taxes. The Company uses significant judgment in (1) determining whether a tax position’s technical merits are more likely than not to be sustained and (2) measuring the amount of tax benefit that qualifies for recognition. As of December 31, 2022, the Company reported accrued liabilities of $4.0 billion for various tax contingencies.
Auditing the recognition and measurement of the Company’s tax contingencies was challenging because the evaluation of whether a tax position is more likely than not to be sustained and the measurement of the benefit of various tax positions can be complex and involves significant auditor judgment. Management’s evaluation of tax positions is based on interpretations of tax laws and legal rulings, and may be impacted by regulatory changes and judicial and examination activity.
How We Addressed the Matter in Our Audit
We tested controls over the Company’s process to assess the technical merits of its tax contingencies, including controls over: the assessment as to whether a tax position is more likely than not to be sustained; the measurement of the benefit of its tax positions, both initially and on an ongoing basis; and the development of the related disclosures.
We involved our international tax, transfer pricing, and research and development tax professionals in assessing the technical merits of certain of the Company’s tax positions. Depending on the nature of the specific tax position and, as applicable, developments with the relevant tax authorities relating thereto, our procedures included obtaining and examining the Company’s analysis including the Company’s correspondence with such tax authorities and evaluating the underlying facts upon which the tax positions are based. We used our knowledge of and experience with international, transfer pricing, and other income tax laws of the relevant taxing jurisdictions to evaluate the Company’s accounting for its tax contingencies. We evaluated developments in the applicable regulatory environments to assess potential effects on the Company’s positions, including recent decisions in relevant court cases. We analyzed the appropriateness of the Company’s assumptions and the accuracy of the Company’s calculations and data used to determine the amount of tax benefits to recognize. We evaluated the Company’s income tax disclosures in relation to these matters.

+28 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"Other - Other revenue includes sales related to various other offerings, such as certain licensing and distribution of video content and shipping services, and our co-branded credit card agreements."
  • jump to section"Revenue is recognized when content is licensed or distributed and as or when services are performed."
  • jump to section"A description of our principal revenue generating activities is as follows: Retail sales - We offer consumer products through our online and physical stores."
  • jump to section"Revenue is recognized when control of the goods is transferred to the customer, which generally occurs upon our delivery to a third-party carrier or, in the case of an Amazon delivery, to the customer."
  • jump to section"Revenue is allocated to services using stand-alone selling prices and is primarily recognized when the customer uses these services, based on the quantity of services rendered, such as compute or storage capacity delivered on-demand."
  • jump to section"Our total unearned revenue as of December 31, 2021 was $ 14.0 billion, of which $ 11.3 billion was recognized as revenue during the year ended December 31, 2022 and our total unearned revenue as of December 31, 2022 was $ 16.1 billion."

Item 1A.Risk Factors
  • jump to section"Our revenue and operating profit growth depends on the continued growth of demand for the products and services offered by us or our sellers, and our business is affected by general economic, business, and geopolitical conditions worldwide."
Legal Proceedings
Legal ProceedingsItem 3.Legal Proceedings jump to section
Item 3.Legal Proceedings
Topic 2 of 2

AMZN: cost of revenue

4 quarter(s) searched · 66 filing section(s) searched · 4 period(s) with evidence
Credibility timeline
Q1 2022Q2 2022Q3 2022Q4 2022
guidance met/exceededguidance missedguidance given, not yet resolvedno forecast data this quarter

Longitudinal Summary

Throughout the period, the cost of revenue increased due to factors such as higher product and shipping costs, increased investments in fulfillment network, transportation costs, and wage rates. Management consistently highlighted efforts to improve cost structures and drive efficiencies. However, the actual cost of sales often exceeded the guidance provided, indicating challenges in accurately forecasting and controlling costs.

Forecasting Credibility: Low

Management's forecasting credibility for the cost of revenue is rated as low, as they consistently missed their guidance on this metric across multiple quarters.

Trends

  • Cost of revenue increased over the period
  • Management focused on improving cost structures and driving efficiencies

Recurring Themes / Risks

  • Increased product and shipping costs
  • Investments in fulfillment network and transportation costs
  • Challenges in controlling and forecasting costs

Key Concerns

  • Consistent misses in cost of revenue guidance indicate a lack of accuracy in forecasting and controlling costs

Conclusion

The cost of revenue trended upwards over the period, driven by various factors such as increased product and shipping costs. Despite management's focus on improving cost structures and driving efficiencies, the actual cost of sales consistently exceeded the guidance provided, indicating challenges in accurately forecasting and controlling costs. This inconsistency raises concerns about the effectiveness of cost management strategies and the reliability of future cost projections.

By Reporting Period

Q1 2022

What management said

  • "And with demand normalizing, we remain focused on rightsizing our cost structure and driving out any cost inefficiencies."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • from 10-K/10-Q"Cost of sales $ 62,403 $ 66,499"
  • from 10-K/10-Q"Cost of sales primarily consists of the purchase price of consumer products, inbound and outbound shipping costs, including costs related to sortation and delivery centers and where we are the transportation service provider, and digital media content costs where we record revenue gross, including video and music."
  • "We'll break these into two buckets, externally driven costs, primarily inflation; and internally controllable costs, primarily productivity and fixed cost deleverage."
  • "We've seen a large cost to keep up with demand these past two years."
  • "When you combine the impacts of the externally driven costs and the internally controllable costs, you get approximately $6 billion in incremental costs for the quarter."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

+1 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"As a measure of sensitivity, for every 1% of additional inventory valuation allowance as of March 31, 2022, we would have recorded an additional cost of sales of approximately $390 million."
  • jump to section"The increase in cost of sales in absolute dollars in Q1 2022, compared to the comparable prior year period, is primarily due to increased product and shipping costs resulting from increased sales, increased investments in our fulfillment network, as well as increased transportation costs, increased wage rates and incentives, and fulfillment network inefficiencies resulting from a constrained labor market and global supply chain constraints."
  • jump to section"Shipping costs to receive products from our suppliers are included in our inventory and recognized as cost of sales upon sale of products to our customers."
  • jump to section"The increase in AWS operating income in absolute dollars in Q1 2022, compared to the comparable prior year period, is primarily due to increased customer usage and cost structure productivity, including a reduction in depreciation and amortization expense from our change in the estimated useful lives of our servers and networking equipment, partially offset by increased spending on technology infrastructure and payroll and related expenses, all of which were primarily driven by additional investments to support the business growth, and reduced prices for our customers."
  • jump to section"Product sales represent revenue from the sale of products and related shipping fees and digital media content where we record revenue gross."

  • jump to section"Our total unearned revenue as of December 31, 2021 was $ 14.0 billion, of which $ 5.1 billion was recognized as revenue during the three months ended March 31, 2022."
  • jump to section"Unearned revenue primarily relates to prepayments of AWS services and Amazon Prime memberships."
  • jump to section"However, the amount and timing of revenue recognition is largely driven by customer usage, which can extend beyond the original contractual term."
Risk Factors
Risk FactorsItem 1A.Risk Factors jump to section
Item 1A.Risk Factors
  • jump to section"Our revenue and operating profit growth depends on the continued growth of demand for the products and services offered by us or our sellers, and our business is affected by general economic and business conditions worldwide."
  • jump to section"For example, non-U.S. jurisdictions have proposed or enacted taxes on online advertising and marketplace service revenues."
Legal Proceedings
Legal ProceedingsItem 1.Legal Proceedings jump to section
Item 1.Legal Proceedings
Q2 2022

What management said

  • "It sounds like you're going to have revenue up nicely."
  • "In our established international locations, UK, Germany, Japan, over time, we've continued to improve the profitability of that business as we build out and established stronger customer relationships and work on the cost structure and how we serve folks."

Guidance given

  • (value: $1.5 billion) "This third quarter guidance assumes, we see approximately $1.5 billion in quarter-over-quarter sequential cost improvement in our fulfillment network operations, which we expect will be largely offset by investments in AWS and additional digital content for Prime members."

Actual reported

  • from 10-K/10-Q"Cost of sales $ 64,176 $ 66,424"
  • from 10-K/10-Q"The increase in cost of sales in absolute dollars in Q2 2022, and for the six months ended June 30, 2022, compared to the comparable prior year periods, is primarily due to increased product and shipping costs resulting from increased sales, increased investments in our fulfillment network, as well as increased transportation costs, increased wage rates and incentives, and fulfillment network inefficiencies resulting from regional labor market and global supply chain constraints."
  • "Other cost pressures are principally on our cost of employees."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

+1 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"As a measure of sensitivity, for every 1% of additional inventory valuation allowance as of June 30, 2022, we would have recorded an additional cost of sales of approximately $425 million."
  • jump to section"The increase in cost of sales in absolute dollars in Q2 2022, and for the six months ended June 30, 2022, compared to the comparable prior year periods, is primarily due to increased product and shipping costs resulting from increased sales, increased investments in our fulfillment network, as well as increased transportation costs, increased wage rates and incentives, and fulfillment network inefficiencies resulting from regional labor market and global supply chain constraints."
  • jump to section"Shipping costs to receive products from our suppliers are included in our inventory and recognized as cost of sales upon sale of products to our customers."
  • jump to section"The increase in AWS operating income in absolute dollars in Q2 2022 and for the six months ended June 30, 2022, compared to the comparable prior year periods, is primarily due to increased customer usage and cost structure productivity, including a reduction in depreciation and amortization expense from our change in the estimated useful lives of our servers and networking equipment, partially offset by increased payroll and related expenses and spending on technology infrastructure, all of which were primarily driven by additional investments to support AWS business growth, and reduced prices for our customers."
  • jump to section"Product sales represent revenue from the sale of products and related shipping fees and digital media content where we record revenue gross."

+11 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"Our total unearned revenue as of December 31, 2021 was $ 14.0 billion, of which $ 8.1 billion was recognized as revenue during the six months ended June 30, 2022."
  • jump to section"Unearned revenue primarily relates to prepayments of AWS services and Amazon Prime memberships."
  • jump to section"However, the amount and timing of revenue recognition is largely driven by customer usage, which can extend beyond the original contractual term."
Risk Factors
Risk FactorsItem 1A.Risk Factors jump to section
Item 1A.Risk Factors
  • jump to section"Our revenue and operating profit growth depends on the continued growth of demand for the products and services offered by us or our sellers, and our business is affected by general economic and business conditions worldwide."
  • jump to section"For example, non-U.S. jurisdictions have proposed or enacted taxes on online advertising and marketplace service revenues."
Legal Proceedings
Legal ProceedingsItem 1.Legal Proceedings jump to section
Item 1.Legal Proceedings
Q3 2022

What management said

  • "First, could you provide some more details on the cost structure initiatives, and when we could see those initiatives hitting the P&L?"
  • "On the cost structure initiatives, I think you're primarily talking about the operations world."
  • "So we have to get our cost structure back to pre-pandemic levels in a lot of areas of the company and mostly in operations."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • Missedfrom 10-K/10-Qresolves AMZN_Q2_2022 guidance "Cost of sales $ 62,930 $ 70,268"
  • from 10-K/10-Q"The increase in cost of sales in absolute dollars for the nine months ended September 30, 2022, compared to the comparable prior year period, is primarily due to increased product and shipping costs resulting from increased sales, increased investments in our fulfillment network, increased transportation costs, increased wage rates and incentives, and fulfillment network inefficiencies."
  • "Turning first to our North America and international segments, during the quarter, we generated over $1 billion in operations cost improvements driven by higher leverage of our fixed cost base and continued productivity improvements in our fulfillment and transportation networks."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

+1 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"As a measure of sensitivity, for every 1% of additional inventory valuation allowance as of September 30, 2022, we would have recorded an additional cost of sales of approximately $405 million."
  • jump to section"The increase in cost of sales in absolute dollars for the nine months ended September 30, 2022, compared to the comparable prior year period, is primarily due to increased product and shipping costs resulting from increased sales, increased investments in our fulfillment network, increased transportation costs, increased wage rates and incentives, and fulfillment network inefficiencies."
  • jump to section"The increase in cost of sales in absolute dollars in Q3 2022, compared to the comparable prior year period, is primarily due to increased product and shipping costs resulting from increased sales, increased investments in our fulfillment network, increased transportation costs, and increased wage rates."
  • jump to section"Shipping costs to receive products from our suppliers are included in our inventory and recognized as cost of sales upon sale of products to our customers."
  • jump to section"The increase in AWS operating income in absolute dollars in Q3 2022 and for the nine months ended September 30, 2022, compared to the comparable prior year periods, is primarily due to increased sales and cost structure productivity, including a reduction in depreciation and amortization expense from our change in the estimated useful lives of our servers and networking equipment, partially offset by increased payroll and related expenses and spending on technology infrastructure, all of which were primarily driven by additional investments to support AWS business growth.Changes in foreign exchange rates positively impacted operating income by $478 million for Q3 2022, and by $976 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2022."

+11 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"Our total unearned revenue as of December 31, 2021 was $ 14.0 billion, of which $ 10.1 billion was recognized as revenue during the nine months ended September 30, 2022."
  • jump to section"Unearned revenue primarily relates to prepayments of AWS services and Amazon Prime memberships."
  • jump to section"However, the amount and timing of revenue recognition is largely driven by customer usage, which can extend beyond the original contractual term."
Risk Factors
Risk FactorsItem 1A.Risk Factors jump to section
Item 1A.Risk Factors
  • jump to section"Our revenue and operating profit growth depends on the continued growth of demand for the products and services offered by us or our sellers, and our business is affected by general economic and business conditions worldwide."
  • jump to section"For example, non-U.S. jurisdictions have proposed or enacted taxes on online advertising and marketplace service revenues."
Legal Proceedings
Legal ProceedingsItem 1.Legal Proceedings jump to section
Item 1.Legal Proceedings
Q4 2022

What management said

  • "This impact is primarily recorded in cost of sales on our income statement."
  • "As a reminder, these digital video content costs are included in cost of sales on our income statement."
  • "Let's start with revenue."
  • "We're just starting to roll out one-day shipping in North America, and we had an expectation of what our cost structure would look like."
  • "On the expectation for retail margins, especially in North America, what we've said is when we look back to our cost structure pre-pandemic, we were just in the end of 2019, early part of 2020."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • from 10-K/10-Q"Cost of sales $ 272,344 $ 288,831"
  • from 10-K/10-Q"The increase in cost of sales in absolute dollars in 2022, compared to the prior year, is primarily due to increased shipping and product costs resulting from increased sales and increases in investments in our fulfillment network, transportation costs, and wage rates and incentives."
  • "The operating income was negatively impacted by 3 large items, which added approximately $2.7 billion of costs in the quarter."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

  • jump to section"As a measure of sensitivity, for every 1% of additional inventory valuation allowance as of December 31, 2022, we would have recorded an additional cost of sales of approximately $390 million."
  • jump to section"The increase in cost of sales in absolute dollars in 2022, compared to the prior year, is primarily due to increased shipping and product costs resulting from increased sales and increases in investments in our fulfillment network, transportation costs, and wage rates and incentives."
  • jump to section"Shipping costs to receive products from our suppliers are included in our inventory and recognized as cost of sales upon sale of products to our customers."
  • jump to section"We also expect the current macroeconomic environment and enterprise customer cost optimization efforts to impact our AWS revenue growth rates."
  • jump to section"The increase in AWS operating income in absolute dollars in 2022, compared to the prior year, is primarily due to increased sales and cost structure productivity, including a reduction in depreciation and amortization expense from our change in the estimated useful lives of our servers and networking equipment, partially offset by increased payroll and related expenses and spending on technology infrastructure, all of which were primarily driven by additional investments to support AWS business growth."

Financial StatementsItem 8.Financial Statements and Supplementary Data jump to section
Uncertain Tax Positions
Description of
the Matter
As discussed in Notes 1 and 9 of the consolidated financial statements, the Company is subject to income taxes in the U.S. and numerous foreign jurisdictions and during the ordinary course of business, there are many tax positions for which the ultimate tax determination is uncertain. As a result, significant judgment is required in evaluating the Company’s tax positions and determining its provision for income taxes. The Company uses significant judgment in (1) determining whether a tax position’s technical merits are more likely than not to be sustained and (2) measuring the amount of tax benefit that qualifies for recognition. As of December 31, 2022, the Company reported accrued liabilities of $4.0 billion for various tax contingencies.
Auditing the recognition and measurement of the Company’s tax contingencies was challenging because the evaluation of whether a tax position is more likely than not to be sustained and the measurement of the benefit of various tax positions can be complex and involves significant auditor judgment. Management’s evaluation of tax positions is based on interpretations of tax laws and legal rulings, and may be impacted by regulatory changes and judicial and examination activity.
How We Addressed the Matter in Our Audit
We tested controls over the Company’s process to assess the technical merits of its tax contingencies, including controls over: the assessment as to whether a tax position is more likely than not to be sustained; the measurement of the benefit of its tax positions, both initially and on an ongoing basis; and the development of the related disclosures.
We involved our international tax, transfer pricing, and research and development tax professionals in assessing the technical merits of certain of the Company’s tax positions. Depending on the nature of the specific tax position and, as applicable, developments with the relevant tax authorities relating thereto, our procedures included obtaining and examining the Company’s analysis including the Company’s correspondence with such tax authorities and evaluating the underlying facts upon which the tax positions are based. We used our knowledge of and experience with international, transfer pricing, and other income tax laws of the relevant taxing jurisdictions to evaluate the Company’s accounting for its tax contingencies. We evaluated developments in the applicable regulatory environments to assess potential effects on the Company’s positions, including recent decisions in relevant court cases. We analyzed the appropriateness of the Company’s assumptions and the accuracy of the Company’s calculations and data used to determine the amount of tax benefits to recognize. We evaluated the Company’s income tax disclosures in relation to these matters.

+28 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"Cost of Sales Cost of sales primarily consists of the purchase price of consumer products, inbound and outbound shipping costs, including costs related to sortation and delivery centers and where we are the transportation service provider, and digital media content costs where we record revenue gross, including video and music."
  • jump to section"Return Allowances Return allowances, which reduce revenue and cost of sales, are estimated using historical experience."
  • jump to section"We also develop original video content for which the production costs are capitalized and amortized to “Cost of sales” predominantly on an accelerated basis that follows the estimated viewing patterns associated with the content."
  • jump to section"We reduce the liability as payments are made and we amortize the asset to “Cost of sales” on an accelerated basis, based on estimated usage or viewing patterns, or on a straight-line basis."
  • jump to section"Shipping costs to receive products from our suppliers are included in our inventory, and recognized as cost of sales upon sale of products to our customers."
  • jump to section"Revenue is allocated to services using stand-alone selling prices and is primarily recognized when the customer uses these services, based on the quantity of services rendered, such as compute or storage capacity delivered on-demand."
Risk Factors
Risk FactorsItem 1A.Risk Factors jump to section
Item 1A.Risk Factors
  • jump to section"For example, non-U.S. jurisdictions have proposed or enacted taxes on online advertising and marketplace service revenues."
